summary: Iteration 2 retest. Backend: 20/20 pytest pass (auth, projects, nodes/edges, repo connect+scan octocat/Hello-World branch=master, Claude AI expand + generate-prompt, exports md/json/agent_pack, node-types, 401 enforcement). Frontend: Inspector crash is FIXED — dblclick sidebar-node-feat mounts inspector with title/content/AI-assist/delete and 0 page errors. Preview mode renders markdown. AI assist 'acceptance_criteria' returns 2.6KB. Command palette adds Technical Architecture node. Console prompt engine and all 3 export formats produce non-empty output. Repo connect to octocat/Hello-World on branch 'master' succeeds (API persists repository.file_tree). Logout redirects to /login. TWO issues found: (1) inspector autosave drops title when title and content are changed within the 350ms debounce window — debounce sends only the latest patch and clears prior pending writes; (2) Repo file tree and framework badges do not render in sidebar UI even though backend returns the tree.

ui_bugs.issue: Autosave debounce drops earlier field patches. persist() calls clearTimeout on each change, then sets a NEW timer that POSTs only the latest patch. If a user changes title and then content within 350ms, the title patch is silently lost and only content is persisted to the backend. Repro: fill title -> immediately fill content -> wait 600ms -> GET /api/nodes returns OLD title with NEW content. Fix: accumulate pending patches in a ref (e.g., pendingRef.current = { ...pendingRef.current, ...patch }) and flush the merged ref on timer fire; reset ref after flush.

After successful POST /repository (200) and scan, repository.file_tree (1 file for octocat/Hello-World) is stored server-side but NO [data-testid^="framework-badge-"] or any file-tree element renders in the sidebar UI. octocat/Hello-World has zero detected frameworks (correct: README-only repo, badges empty is acceptable) BUT the file tree count = 1 must still render so the user can click a file to attach to a selected node. Without this UI, the 'click file to attach to node' workflow is unreachable from the UI.

integration_issues.flow: Attach file to node from repo tree
integration_issues.issue: Cannot exercise because repo file tree never renders in sidebar (see UI bug above). API-level file_references update works (covered by backend tests).
Not exercised — React Flow handle drag is brittle in headless Playwright. Backend POST /edges is covered by pytest. Manual visual smoke recommended.

action_items: FIX Inspector.jsx autosave: accumulate pending patches in a ref instead of replacing on each keystroke so changing title then content does not silently drop the title write.

Render repo file tree (and optionally framework badges section) in Sidebar.jsx after connect+scan so users can click a file to attach to the selected node. Add data-testid="repo-file-<path>" per file row. Also expose framework-badge-* for any detected frameworks (octocat repo has none — pick a JS/Py repo for visual verification).
Project rename via 'project-name-button' on canvas header was not exercised — please verify manually; testid may not exist (was not in our scan).
Logout currently redirects to /login (review request asked for /). Confirm intended UX; otherwise update navigate target in Dashboard logout handler.
critical_code_review_comments: Inspector.jsx persist(): debounce loses field-level patches on rapid edits. Use a merged-pending-patch ref pattern.
Sidebar.jsx repo tab post-connect render path is incomplete — file_tree from API is not iterated into clickable rows.
server.py @app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI 0.110+; switch to lifespan context.
server.py stores GitHub PAT in plaintext on user doc — acceptable for MVP, must encrypt at rest before prod.
Inspector fix (current = (draft && draft.id===node.id) ? draft : node) is clean and works as intended.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — Inspector flow, AI, palette, prompt, exports all working; repo file tree rendering and title autosave race are the gaps

test_credentials: Registered on the fly: e2e+<ts>@cortexflow.dev / cortexflow123 (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md guidance)
seed_data_creation: None persisted — all test projects use ephemeral fresh users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Inspector crash is FIXED — do not waste time re-verifying. Focus next iteration on (1) re-running the title+content rapid-edit autosave test once the persist() patch-merge fix lands, and (2) verifying sidebar repo file tree rendering with a richer repo such as 'vercel/next.js' on branch 'canary' (which will populate frameworks badges and a non-empty file tree). For the React Flow edge drag, prefer a programmatic addEdge via the app's API mock or use page.mouse.down/move/up over .react-flow__handle.source coordinates obtained via boundingBox.
rca of the issue: Autosave race: persist() in Inspector.jsx uses clearTimeout + setTimeout with a single patch object. Each new keystroke replaces the pending patch entirely; the earlier {title: ...} is lost when the next {content: ...} replaces it before the 350ms fires. Fix: store the accumulated patch in a useRef and merge new patches into it; flush the ref on timer fire (then clear). Repo UI: Sidebar.jsx renders connect-repo-button and framework-badge-* only when frameworks > 0, and does not iterate repository.file_tree into clickable rows — main agent must add a file tree list with data-testid="repo-file-<path>" and an onClick that calls onChange(selectedNode.id, { file_references: [...existing, path] }).
